§6Vitriol§f

§aViscous byproduct of bedrock ore roasting — process it for mercury, iron, sulfuric acid, and chlorine!§f

Vitriol is a viscous liquid produced by roasting any bedrock ore fragment in the §bCombination Oven§f or §bPyrolysis Oven§f. Every roasted fragment yields §e50mB§f Vitriol as byproduct. It is a key intermediate for §bMercury§f production and §bSulfuric Acid§f recovery, essential for automating your ore processing lines.

§6Production§f:
§b• Combination Oven§f: Any bedrock ore fragment → Roasted Fragment + §e50mB§f Vitriol.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: Any bedrock ore fragment → Roasted Fragment + §e50mB§f Vitriol.

§6Uses§f:
§b• SILEX§f: Vitriol + CO₂-Desh Laser Crystal → Bromine Powder (§e16.6%§f), Iron Powder (§e16.6%§f), Iodine Powder (§e16.6%§f), Sulfur (§e50%§f).
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e4,000mB§f Vitriol → Iron Powder + §e500mB§f Sulfuric Acid.
§b• Electrolysis Machine§f: §e1,000mB§f Vitriol → §e500mB§f Sulfuric Acid + §e500mB§f Chlorine Gas + Iron Powder + Drop of Mercury.

§eTip§f: Vitriol is the most reliable renewable source of §bMercury§f in the mod. Processing it through the Electrolysis Machine yields a Drop of Mercury plus Sulfuric Acid and Chlorine Gas — three valuable outputs from one byproduct. Never discard your vitriol!
